questions.question: What CSS technique creates a ring spinner effect?
options: Border with transparent side and rotation
questions.explanation: A ring spinner uses a border with one solid-colored side and rotates the element.

questions.question: How do you create a shimmer effect on skeletons?
questions.explanation: Shimmer moves a gradient across the element using background-position animation.

questions.question: What role attribute should loading indicators have?
questions.explanation: role="status" is appropriate for live loading indicators.

questions.question: How do you stagger dot animations for a typing effect?
questions.explanation: Each dot gets a different animation-delay via nth-child selectors.

questions.question: Which property is best for animating a progress bar?
questions.explanation: scaleX is GPU accelerated; width triggers layout.

questions.question: What is the simplest CSS-only spinner?
questions.explanation: A single element with a border and rotation animation is the simplest spinner.

questions.question: How do you ensure loading animations don't cause performance issues?
questions.explanation: GPU-accelerated properties (transform, opacity) ensure smooth performance.

questions.question: What does aria-label="Loading" do?
questions.explanation: aria-label provides accessible text for screen readers.

questions.question: Which animation timing is best for a smooth spinner?
questions.explanation: linear timing is best for spinners because constant speed looks most natural for rotation.

questions.question: How do you create a pulsing skeleton placeholder?
questions.explanation: Pulsing skeletons animate opacity to create a breathing/loading effect.

questions.question: What is the difference between determinate and indeterminate progress?
options: Determinate shows completion %, indeterminate shows activity without known duration
Determinate is for spinners, indeterminate is for bars
Determinate uses CSS, indeterminate uses JS
questions.explanation: Determinate progress shows a known completion percentage; indeterminate indicates activity without known duration.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of skeleton screens over spinners?
options: Improved perceived performance by showing content layout
questions.explanation: Skeleton screens improve perceived performance by giving users a sense of progress and familiar layout.

questions.question: How do you create an accessible loading spinner?
questions.explanation: role="status" and screen-reader-only text ensure accessibility.

questions.question: What is the recommended size for inline UI spinners?
questions.explanation: Keep spinners under 40px for inline UI elements to avoid visual distraction.

questions.question: Which is more GPU-intensive: shimmer or opacity pulse?
questions.explanation: Shimmer triggers paint; opacity pulse is simpler and more performant.

questions.question: What is the keyframe for a ring spinner rotation?
options: from { transform: rotate(0deg); } to { transform: rotate(360deg); }
questions.explanation: A spinner rotates from 0 to 360 degrees using transform: rotate().

questions.question: Which aria attribute should a skeleton container have?
questions.explanation: aria-busy="true" on the container indicates content is still loading.

questions.question: What should you animate for a dot-loader bounce effect?
questions.explanation: Scale or translateY on dots creates the bounce effect without triggering layout.

questions.question: How do you create an indeterminate progress bar animation?
options: Animate translateX on a pseudo-element sliding back and forth
questions.explanation: An indeterminate bar uses translateX to slide a bar back and forth endlessly.

questions.question: What should you do when content finishes loading?
options: Hide the loader with display:none or remove it from DOM
questions.explanation: Always remove or hide loading indicators once content is ready.
